Title: Gene duplication and clustering underlie the conservation and diversification of benzylisoquinoline alkaloid biosynthesis in plants

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Houttuynia cordata / Nicotiana benthamiana transient expression system
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as rate-limiting for 1-BIA production based on prior studies cited in paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes coclaurine formation and supports core 1-BIA backbone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmacologically significant specialized metabolites with analgesic, antiseptic, and antineoplastic activities
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Rhizomes (predominant 1-BIA accumulation in H. cordata); also leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Candidate pathway genes showed similar expression profiles across six tissues; specific Hc6OMT1 pattern not individually resolved in text
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient assay in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves with exogenous NCC feeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; substrate feeding; UPLC-MS product detection; crude protein in vitro methyltransferase assay with SAM
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Hc6OMT1 converted supplied norcoclaurine into coclaurine in transient tobacco assays. 2. Its product matched authentic COC by retention time and MS spectra. 3. In vitro crude protein assays confirmed methyltransferase activity, supporting a conserved early 1-BIA pathway role in H. cordata.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Published Houttuynia cordata transcriptome data24 used for expression matrix (FPKM); genome-guided homology search; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-XhoI transient expression vector; promoter not stated
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for reconstructing conserved upstream BIA backbone biosynthesis and for synthetic biology production of reticuline-derived BIAs; may be stacked with CNMT, CYP80B and 4'OMT
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of two functional H. cordata 6OMT paralogs; gene clustering analyses indicate 6OMT often colocalizes with CYP80B/4'OMT across angiosperms

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Houttuynia cordata / Nicotiana benthamiana transient expression system
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as rate-limiting for 1-BIA production based on prior studies cited in paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes coclaurine formation and supports core 1-BIA backbone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmacologically significant specialized metabolites with analgesic, antiseptic, and antineoplastic activities
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Rhizomes (predominant 1-BIA accumulation in H. cordata); also leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Candidate pathway genes showed similar expression profiles across six tissues; specific Hc6OMT2 pattern not individually resolved in text
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient assay in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves with exogenous NCC feeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; substrate feeding; UPLC-MS product detection; crude protein in vitro methyltransferase assay with SAM
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Hc6OMT2 converted supplied norcoclaurine into coclaurine in transient tobacco assays. 2. Product identity matched authentic COC standard. 3. Crude protein assays confirmed methyltransferase activity, indicating a second functional 6OMT paralog in H. cordata.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Published Houttuynia cordata transcriptome data24 used for expression matrix (FPKM); genome-guided homology search; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-XhoI transient expression vector; promoter not stated
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for pathway reconstruction and possible redundancy/robustness engineering in upstream BIA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paralog of Hc6OMT1; supports gene duplication underlying pathway conservation/diversification

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Houttuynia cordata / Nicotiana benthamiana transient expression system
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Mainstream 1-BIA backbone formation; methylation branch toward NMC and MGC
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor toward (S)-reticuline and also forms side-product magnocurarine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: N-methylates coclaurine to produce N-methylcoclaurine and can further di-methylate to magnocurarine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes NMC formation for reticuline biosynthesis and also contributes to MGC production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Rhizomes (predominant 1-BIA accumulation in H. cordata); also leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Candidate pathway genes largely shared similar expression profiles across six tissues; gene-specific pattern not detailed
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient assay in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves with exogenous COC feeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; substrate feeding; UPLC-MS detection of NMC and MGC; crude protein in vitro methyltransferase assay with SAM
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HcNMT2 produced NMC from COC in transient tobacco assays. 2. HcNMT2 also generated di-methylated MGC, indicating broader catalytic capacity in Magnoliids. 3. In vitro assays confirmed methyltransferase activity.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Published Houttuynia cordata transcriptome data24 used for expression matrix (FPKM); genome-guided homology search; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-XhoI transient expression vector; promoter not stated
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Flux partitioning toward MGC may divert precursor from RTC branch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to reconstruct NMC supply for reticuline biosynthesis, but possible competing MGC branch should be considered in engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of three H. cordata NMTs retaining NMC-producing activity

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Houttuynia cordata / Nicotiana benthamiana transient expression system
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes NMC formation for reticuline biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Rhizomes (predominant 1-BIA accumulation in H. cordata); also leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Candidate pathway genes largely shared similar expression profiles across six tissues; gene-specific pattern not detailed
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient assay in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves with exogenous COC feeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; substrate feeding; UPLC-MS detection of NMC; crude protein in vitro methyltransferase assay with SAM
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HcNMT3 produced NMC from COC in transient tobacco assays. 2. It was validated as a functional CNMT-type enzyme in H. cordata. 3. In vitro assays supported methyltransferase activity.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Published Houttuynia cordata transcriptome data24 used for expression matrix (FPKM); genome-guided homology search; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-XhoI transient expression vector; promoter not stated
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for NMC supply in engineered reticuline production pathways
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike HcNMT2/HcNMT4/HcNMT5, MGC formation was not reported for HcNMT3 in text

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Houttuynia cordata / Nicotiana benthamiana transient expression system
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Mainstream 1-BIA backbone formation; methylation branch toward NMC and MGC
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor toward (S)-reticuline and also forms side-product magnocurarine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: N-methylates coclaurine to produce N-methylcoclaurine and can also generate di-methylated magnocurarine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes NMC formation for reticuline biosynthesis and contributes to MGC production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Rhizomes (predominant 1-BIA accumulation in H. cordata); also leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Candidate pathway genes largely shared similar expression profiles across six tissues; gene-specific pattern not detailed
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient assay in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves with exogenous COC feeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; substrate feeding; UPLC-MS detection of NMC and MGC; crude protein in vitro methyltransferase assay with SAM
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HcNMT4 produced NMC from COC in transient tobacco assays. 2. HcNMT4 also generated MGC, indicating dual methylation capability. 3. In vitro assays confirmed methyltransferase function.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Published Houttuynia cordata transcriptome data24 used for expression matrix (FPKM); genome-guided homology search; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-XhoI transient expression vector; promoter not stated
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Flux partitioning toward MGC may compete with RTC branch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potentially useful for flexible engineering of either NMC/RTC or MGC-related branches depending pathway context
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional paralog with broader catalytic role than canonical Ranunculales CNMTs

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Houttuynia cordata / Nicotiana benthamiana transient expression system
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Benzylisoquinoline alkaloid biosynthesis; MGC branch derived from coclaurine
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Alternative methylation branch from coclaurine to magnocurarine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: diverts flux toward alternative di-methylated product rather than RTC
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Specifically di-methylates coclaurine-derived intermediate(s) to generate magnocurarine without detectable NMC accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Specifically promotes MGC biosynthesis and exemplifies neofunctionalization after gene duplication
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Benzylisoquinoline alkaloid; specific biological function not described in paper
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Candidate pathway genes largely shared similar expression profiles across six tissues; gene-specific pattern not detailed
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient assay in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves with exogenous COC feeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; substrate feeding; UPLC-MS detection of exclusive MGC formation; crude protein in vitro methyltransferase assay; sequence alignment identifying key glycine residue
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HcNMT5 did not produce NMC from COC but exclusively formed the di-methylated product MGC. 2. Sequence alignment showed a glycine at the key catalytic position associated with di-methylation specificity. 3. The study identifies HcNMT5 as a naturally neofunctionalized enzyme underlying a complex MGC biosynthetic network in H. cordata.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Published Houttuynia cordata transcriptome data24 used for expression matrix (FPKM); genome-guided homology search; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-XhoI transient expression vector; promoter not stated
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May divert coclaurine away from reticuline biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable for engineering selective MGC production; should be avoided or downregulated when maximizing reticuline flux
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted by authors as neofunctionalized NMT; paper text likely contains typo calling it natural NCC di-methyltransferase though assays supplied COC

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Houttuynia cordata / Nicotiana benthamiana transient expression system
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates N-methylcoclaurine at the 3' position to generate 3'-hydroxy-NMC
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables conversion of NMC toward reticuline and is part of a functional HcCYP80B-Hc4'OMT1 mini-gene cluster
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Central branch-point intermediate for diverse pharmacologically significant BIAs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Rhizomes (predominant 1-BIA accumulation in H. cordata); also leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly similar expression pattern to clustered Hc4'OMT1 across major tissues
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient assay in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves with exogenous NMC feeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; substrate feeding; UPLC-MS detection of hydroxylated intermediate; co-expression with Hc4'OMT1 to confirm product identity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HcCYP80B converted supplied NMC into a product 16 mass units higher, identified as 3'-hydroxy-NMC. 2. Co-expression with Hc4'OMT1 sharply decreased this intermediate and produced RTC. 3. HcCYP80B is tightly linked and co-expressed with Hc4'OMT1, defining a functional reticuline mini-gene cluster in H. cordata.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Published Houttuynia cordata transcriptome data24 used for expression matrix (FPKM); genome-guided homology search; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-XhoI transient expression vector; promoter not stated
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core enzyme for reconstructing reticuline biosynthesis; clustered organization may aid marker-assisted discovery of BIA loci
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of widespread dynamic CYP80B-4'OMT or CYP80B-6OMT clusters across Magnoliids and Ranunculales

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Houttuynia cordata / Nicotiana benthamiana transient expression system
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream; terminal step of RTC formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly promotes RTC production and functions with clustered HcCYP80B in the NMC-to-RTC conversion module
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Central branch-point intermediate for diverse pharmacologically significant BIAs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Rhizomes (predominant 1-BIA accumulation in H. cordata); also leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly similar expression pattern to HcCYP80B across major tissues
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient assay in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves with exogenous NMC feeding and co-expression with HcCYP80B
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with HcCYP80B plus substrate feeding; UPLC-MS detection of RTC
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Co-expression of Hc4'OMT1 with HcCYP80B converted the hydroxylated intermediate into RTC. 2. Hc4'OMT1 is tightly linked to HcCYP80B and shares highly similar expression patterns. 3. The HcCYP80B-Hc4'OMT1 mini-gene cluster drives NMC-to-RTC conversion in H. cordata.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Published Houttuynia cordata transcriptome data24 used for expression matrix (FPKM); genome-guided homology search; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-XhoI transient expression vector; promoter not stated
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value component for direct RTC biosynthesis and synthetic BIA platforms; clustered context may facilitate pathway mining
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Primary expressed 4'OMT paralog in H. cordata

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Houttuynia cordata / Nicotiana benthamiana transient expression system
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream; terminal step of RTC formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Can support RTC formation but appears less efficient and is negligibly expressed across tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Central branch-point intermediate for diverse pharmacologically significant BIAs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Rhizomes (predominant 1-BIA accumulation in H. cordata); also leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Negligible expression across all analyzed tissue transcriptomes
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient assay in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves with exogenous NMC feeding and co-expression context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana with substrate feeding; UPLC-MS product detection in co-expression assays
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Published Houttuynia cordata transcriptome data24 used for expression matrix (FPKM); genome-guided homology search; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-XhoI transient expression vector; promoter not stated
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May serve as an alternative enzyme in heterologous engineering, but low native expression suggests lesser physiological contribution
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Multiple CYP80B copies flanking Hc4'OMT2 are likely pseudogenizing

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Houttuynia cordata / Nicotiana benthamiana transient expression system
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Benzylisoquinoline alkaloid biosynthesis; aporphine alkaloid branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target aporphine product from reticuline
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes intramolecular C8-C6' phenol coupling of reticuline to form isoboldine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly produces isoboldine and defines the first reported plant isoboldine synthase
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Aporphine alkaloid; specific biological or pharmacological function not described in paper
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves and rhizomes of H. cordata
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient assay in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves with exogenous RTC feeding; in vitro assay with RTC and NADPH
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; substrate feeding; UPLC-MS/MS comparison to isoboldine standard; detection of IBD in H. cordata tissues; crude protein in vitro enzyme assay; AlphaFold3 structure prediction and docking simulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HcCYP80G2 converted supplied RTC into a product matching isoboldine standard by retention time and fragmentation. 2. IBD was detected in H. cordata leaves and rhizomes, supporting in planta relevance. 3. In vitro crude protein assays confirmed only HcCYP80G2, not HcCYP80G1, produces IBD. 4. Docking supported a mechanistic model for C8-C6' phenol coupling.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: AlphaFold3 structural prediction; published Houttuynia cordata genome/transcriptome resources used for candidate identification; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-XhoI transient expression vector; promoter not stated
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Diverts RTC from other downstream BIA branches
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value tool for synthetic biology production of isoboldine and exploration of aporphine scaffold diversification
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: First reported plant enzyme responsible for IBD biosynthesis; highlighted as a neofunctionalized paralog
